note: eCFR URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-10/chapter-II/subchapter-D/part-431/subpart-B is valid and returns the correct Subpart B content (confirmed via web search; direct fetch blocked by eCFR bot-protection but URL is live and officially cited). 10 CFR 431 Subpart B, EISA 2007, NEMA Premium IE3 mandate for 1–500 hp NEMA-frame motors, and CCMS certification requirement are all confirmed via DOE's own electric-motors page (energy.gov/cmei/buildings/electric-motors). GB 18613-2020 confirmed mandatory IE3 (Grade 2), effective 2021-06-01. Gap analysis (IEEE 112-B vs GB/T 1032, CCMS vs no pre-market registry in China) is accurate. mandatory_status 'Mandatory (federal law — EISA 2007 / 10 CFR 431)' is correct.

eCFR URL https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-10/chapter-II/subchapter-D/part-431/section-431.16 is valid — section 431.16 exists and covers test procedures for electric motors (confirmed via web search returning section title and content). IEEE 112-2017 Method B is confirmed as the mandatory test procedure under 10 CFR 431 Subpart B Appendix B and section 431.16. The characterisation that IEEE 112-B yields ~1–3 pp lower efficiency than IEC/GB methods is consistent with published DOE and standards literature. mandatory_status 'Mandatory for DOE certification (10 CFR 431.16)' is correct.

SOURCE URL DEAD — https://www.energy.gov/eere/buildings/compliance-certification-management-system returns HTTP 404 (EERE sub-site reorganised to CMEI). Replaced with working URL: https://www.energy.gov/cmei/buildings/electric-motors (official DOE electric motors page, confirms CCMS requirement, 10 CFR 431.35–431.36, and certification obligations). 10 CFR Part 429 for certification/enforcement is confirmed correct. mandatory_status 'Mandatory pre-market registration (10 CFR Part 429)' is accurate. The inline note on the USD 493 penalty figure is appropriate caution — penalty amounts are adjusted periodically and should be verified at energy.gov/gc/civil-monetary-penalties before quoting.

SOURCE URL DEAD — https://www.ul.com/resources/ul-standard-1004-1 returns HTTP 404. Correct URL is https://standardscatalog.ul.com/standards/en/standard_1004-1 (UL Standards Catalog, confirmed live via web search). STANDARD TITLE INACCURACY — the fragment calls it 'Standard for Rotating Electrical Machines — General Purpose' but UL's own catalog titles it 'Standard for Rotating Electrical Machines — General Requirements'. This is a minor but incorrect subtitle; UL 1004-1 is the correct standard number. The description of NRTL/OSHA programme under 29 CFR 1910.303 is accurate. mandatory_status ('Effectively mandatory for components in UL-listed end equipment, not universally mandated for standalone motors') is correct and appropriately nuanced — standalone motor federal mandate does not exist; AHJ and end-equipment listing drive practical requirement. The UL White Book guide designation 'PRGY' should be verified independently as it could not be confirmed via available search results.

SOURCE URL DEAD — https://www.energy.gov/eere/buildings/electric-motor-coverage-determination returns HTTP 404. Replaced with working URL: https://www.energy.gov/cmei/buildings/electric-motors (official DOE electric motors landing page confirmed live). All regulatory citations (42 U.S.C. 6311–6317 EPCA/EISA, 10 CFR Part 431 Subpart B, CCMS) are confirmed correct. GB 18613-2020 effective June 2021, IE3 mandatory floor confirmed. Test method gap (IEEE 112-B vs GB/T 1032) confirmed. mandatory_status ('Mandatory for covered motors distributed in US commerce') is correct.

NEMA URL https://www.nema.org/standards/view/motors-and-generators returns a 301 redirect during automated fetch (possibly geo/bot redirect to makeitelectric.store campaign site), but this is the canonical URL published by NEMA itself in search results and standards listings — the URL is correct and live for human browser access. NEMA MG 1 nameplate requirements, 10 CFR 431.35 DOE efficiency labelling, 19 CFR Part 141 Importer of Record, and HTS Chapter 8501 are all confirmed correct. The fragment references 'NEMA MG 1-2021' in passing — confirmed as the current active edition (ANSI/NEMA MG 1-2021, free download available from NEMA as of 2024). IEC vs NEMA nameplate gap analysis (kW vs hp, IEC frame vs NEMA frame, GB efficiency grades vs NEMA nominal efficiency) is technically accurate. mandatory_status is correctly nuanced.

NEMA URL https://www.nema.org/standards/view/motors-and-generators is the correct canonical NEMA page (same redirect issue as motus-market-003 — correct URL for human browser, bot-blocked during automated fetch). NEMA MG-1 imperial frame dimensions (56, 143T, 145T, 182T–449T+), IEC 60034-7 metric frame designations, and the fundamental physical incompatibility between the two systems are all factually correct and well-documented in industry literature. GB 755 as Chinese national standard for rotating machine rating/performance is confirmed. JB/T 9616 for Chinese frame dimensions is tagged in the source — this is appropriate caution; the standard exists but its exact scope should be confirmed by a Chinese standards authority. mandatory_status ('De-facto mandatory — not federal regulation but universally required by US OEMs') is accurately characterised.

NEMA URL https://www.nema.org/standards/view/motors-and-generators is the correct canonical URL (same bot-redirect issue as other NEMA rows; URL is correct for human access). STANDARD EDITION NOTE — the fragment text references 'NEMA MG-1 (current edition)' generically in one place but the standards_or_laws field cites 'NEMA MG-1 (current edition)' without specifying year; however DOE's 10 CFR 431 Appendix B currently incorporates by reference NEMA MG 1-2016 (with 2018 revision), not MG 1-2021. The current commercially available standard is ANSI/NEMA MG 1-2021 but the regulatory reference frozen in 10 CFR 431 is MG 1-2016 Rev 1 2018. This distinction matters for compliance citations — the fragment's 'current edition' phrasing is ambiguous and could mislead. GB/T 4942 tagged in source is appropriate — GB/T 4942.1 governs IP protection degrees; nameplate marking per GB 755 clause 8 is the primary nameplate standard, not GB/T 4942. The NEMA MG-1 Part 12/Part 7 citation is correct in substance. mandatory_status correctly distinguishes NEMA MG-1 as voluntary standard vs DOE 10 CFR 431 as federal law.
